title: Data Dictionary for allegheny_dog_licenses.csv
description:
**Description**

This 50 013‑row dataset records dog license information for a single U.S. jurisdiction. Each row is uniquely identified by an integer `_id` and includes the type of license, breed, color, name, owner ZIP code, expiration year (fixed at 2099), and the exact date the license becomes valid. The data are largely categorical, with heavy skew toward a few common license types, mixed breeds, black or brown colors, and older validity dates spanning from 2003 to mid‑2026. Most of the free‑text fields exhibit high cardinality: nearly one‑third of color values and over half of breed values fall into an “Other” bucket, while dog names are largely unique with only a handful appearing frequently.

**Notable Characteristics**

- **Unique identifiers** – The `_id` field is strictly unique; no duplicate records exist.  
- **License type skew** – About 30 % each of the top three license categories dominate the dataset (Lifetime Spayed Female, Lifetime Neutered Male, and Lifetime Male).  
- **Breed & color diversity** – Mixed breeds account for ~11 % of records; over 58 % of breed entries are in an “Other” group. For color, black is most common (~11 %), yet 33 % of values fall under “Other.”  
- **Dog name variability** – Free‑text names have very high cardinality (≈10 k distinct names) with 91 % appearing only once; the top ten names account for <12 % of all records.  
- **ZIP code distribution** – 132 distinct ZIP codes are represented, but 64 % of owners fall into an “Other” category, indicating many less frequent locations.  
- **Expiration year constant** – All licenses expire in 2099, suggesting a perpetual or very long‑term licensing scheme.  
- **Validity dates spread** – Dates range from 2003‑01‑01 to 2026‑07‑01 with a mean of 2017‑09‑19 and median 2014‑01‑17; the distribution is wide (≈47 years) but heavily skewed toward older dates.  
- **Missing data** – Only one null entry appears in the `Breed` field; all other fields are fully populated.  
- **Data quality issues** – The prevalence of “Other” categories and free‑text variability indicates potential for inconsistent terminology and a need for standardization or cleaning.  
- **PII/PHI considerations** – While no owner names or addresses are present, dog names could be considered personal data; the dataset appears anonymized except for ZIP codes, which provide geographic context.

---

_id.title: Unique Identifier
_id.description: A unique numeric key that identifies each dog license record. The value is guaranteed to be distinct across all rows and can be used as a primary key for relational operations.

LicenseType.title: Dog License Type
LicenseType.description: The type of license issued to the dog, indicating its lifetime status and any special conditions such as spaying or neutering. There are 11 distinct categories in the dataset.

Color.title: Dog Color
Color.description: The primary color(s) of the dog expressed by descriptive names such as BLACK, BROWN, or combinations like WHITE/BLACK.

DogName.title: Dog Name
DogName.description: The given name of the dog. Names are free‑text entries and may be repeated across many records.

OwnerZip.title: Owner ZIP Code
OwnerZip.description: Five‑digit postal code of the dog's owner, used to identify geographic location within the United States.

ExpYear.title: Expiration Year
ExpYear.description: The year in which the dog’s license expires. All records currently have the value 2099, indicating a perpetual or very long‑term license.

ValidDate.title: License Validity Date
ValidDate.description: The exact date and time when the dog’s license becomes valid. The value is stored in ISO‑8601 format with UTC offset (e.g., 2003-01-01T00:00:00+00:00).
